steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: ”Taken by P.H. Polk, the photo” is a subject preceded by a participial modifier. After the blank comes “King in profile, the glow of a stage light casting his silhouette onto the curtains behind him”—the object and supporting details. The blank must contain the main verb for this independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the grammatical rule
steps.content: An independent clause requires a finite verb as its main verb. “Shows” (choice C) is the only finite verb option that can serve as the main verb here. The other options—”to show,” “having shown,” and “showing”—are non-finite forms that cannot function as the main verb of an independent clause. That matches choice C — C is correct.
